prompt_queue.prompt: Bootstrap the repository's non-technical, user-facing living documentation from source code. Treat this as a first-run documentation bootstrap. First build a coverage map from README.md, docs/**, source files, UI strings, commands, configuration, tests, examples, issues, and pull requests. Complete bootstrap coverage includes every major user-visible feature, setup path, configuration option, permission or requirement, common task, operational expectation, compatibility boundary, troubleshooting path, and FAQ-worthy behavior, with any intentionally uncovered area explicitly marked out of scope. The default bootstrap output is a documentation pull request that creates or updates a docs index plus separate user task/topic pages and includes the coverage map in committed documentation. Create concise topic pages with clear titles, bounded scope, and cross-links from the index. Explain implementation details when they affect user behavior. Use create_or_update_github_file before create_github_pull_request. If tool, token, or writable-scope limits constrain the pass, state the partial coverage in the committed docs and pull request body. Use no_changes when existing docs already include a complete, navigable user docs surface and an explicit coverage map across all user-visible behavior.
